parsed_review.summary: CP00-530 is a clean descriptor-only closeout pack covering 10 RP17.P04 units (RP17.P04.M05.S15-RP17.P04.M06.S02; M05 Permission And Audit Binding tail of 8 rows + M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head of 2 rows). It follows the established CP514-CP529 descriptor pattern exactly and is internally consistent across registry.js (CP530 pack binding, requirements, no-write attestation), service.js (case-set + descriptor builders), validators.js (coverage/slice/Hermes/Claude/handoff), the 613KB contract JSON projections (current_pack, cp530_requirements, cp530_no_write_attestation, p04_permission_fixture_slice_descriptor, historical_pack_bindings tail), the closeout-pack-plan.json pack entry, manifest.json, model.test.js, README, and the validator script. Distributions match with no drift (M05:8 + M06:2 = 10; implementation:4, fixture:1, test:1, hermes_evidence:1, claude_review:1, ui:2). The pack-level production_ready claim is NOT overstated: manifest declares production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false / implementation_layer:descriptor, and the production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification token only. Every runtime/write/dispatch flag is false and all leak-exposure flags are false; case-set rows are descriptor_only with no raw payloads, no real data, no state writes, no permission evaluation. Authority boundary is preserved: Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer (not final approver), human final approval is required for runtime opening, and no enterprise-trust claim is derived from local validation. Hermes patterns are used solely for the deterministic evidence-packet descriptor (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime. Review-hardening preconditions are satisfied: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1434-byte stdout, no stderr), no forbidden tools, prompt within size limit, no invalid prior review attempts, and changed-file-scope correctly isolates active refs from unrelated dirty UI-workstream files. Post-review artifacts (adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, claude-review-result.json) are legitimately absent because they are produced after this read-only review step in the hardening sequence. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout.
